variants.label: Session Plan
variants.optimizes_for: Isometric core stability and posterior-chain tension under load.

warmup.name: High Plank Bird Dog
warmup.rationale: Activation for the core to stabilize before loaded work.
warmup.sequencing_rationale: Early in the warmup to wake up stabilizers before load.

warmup.name: Walking Toe Touches
warmup.rationale: Primes the hamstrings ahead of the main movement.
warmup.sequencing_rationale: Placed just before the compound it protects.

warmup.name: Ground Upper Trap Stretch
warmup.rationale: Primes the traps ahead of the main movement.
warmup.sequencing_rationale: Placed just before the compound it protects.

main.name: One-Kettlebell Hamstring Walkout
main.rationale: Primary compound — the session's main hamstrings driver.
main.sequencing_rationale: CNS-intensive compound first, while you're freshest.

main.name: Farmers Carry
main.rationale: Primary compound — the session's main forearms driver.
main.sequencing_rationale: CNS-intensive compound first, while you're freshest.

main.name: Dumbbell Suitcase Carry
main.rationale: Primary compound — the session's main core driver.
main.sequencing_rationale: CNS-intensive compound first, while you're freshest.

main.name: Single-Arm Kettlebell Carry (Rack Position)
main.rationale: Primary compound — the session's main core driver.
main.sequencing_rationale: CNS-intensive compound first, while you're freshest.

main.name: Push-Up to Knee-Drive
main.rationale: Primary compound — the session's main chest driver.
main.sequencing_rationale: CNS-intensive compound first, while you're freshest.

cooldown.name: Standing Neck Circles
cooldown.sequencing_rationale: Final — parasympathetic down-regulation to close the session.

plan.stimulus: Isometric core stability and posterior-chain tension under load.
plan.target_adaptation: Proximal strength and anti-rotation resilience; pain-free knee stability.
plan.design_rationale: This session prioritizes lower-body strength through isometric and anti-rotation core work while respecting the 70% load tolerance and deep knee flexion restriction imposed by patellofemoral pain syndrome. The coach selected carries (farmers, suitcase, rack-position kettlebell) and hamstring walkouts as primary strength drivers—all emphasizing posterior chain and proximal stability without demanding deep squat or lunge mechanics. The 40-second work intervals and 180-second rest periods allow adequate recovery between high-tension isometric holds, supporting strength adaptation without accumulating inflammatory load. Push-up to knee-drive is capped at 5 reps to limit knee flexion volume while maintaining dynamic lower-body engagement; the warmup's bird dog and toe touches activate glute and core stabilizers before loading, reducing compensatory stress on the patellofemoral joint.
plan.sequence_logic: Warmup primes glute and core stability with low-impact activation; main work orders carries and hamstring work (minimal knee bend, high stability demand) while the nervous system is fresh, finishing with a controlled knee-drive pattern to reinforce proximal control under fatigue.

decision_trace.name: resolve_prompt
decision_trace.detail: Extract concept terms from the coach's free-text prompt using the 3-pass resolver (exact → fuzzy → embedding).

decision_trace.name: load_constraints
decision_trace.detail: Load member equipment list and dislikes for downstream gates.

decision_trace.name: part_of_traversal
decision_trace.detail: Traverse the SNOMED anatomy part-of hierarchy from 'knee' to collect all descendant anatomical nodes (e.g. patellofemoral joint, tibial plateau) that are part of the injured region.  Exercises stressing any of these nodes are candidates for exclusion.

decision_trace.name: movement_type_exclusion
decision_trace.detail: Exclude exercises that perform any of ['flexion'] at the injured joint 'knee' based on today's pain_on check-in and the active healing phase's movement restrictions.

decision_trace.name: equipment_gate
decision_trace.detail: Remove exercises that require equipment the member does not have.

decision_trace.name: llm_structuring
decision_trace.detail: The LLM receives only the 17 safe exercises and structures them into a single WorkoutPlan — assigning sets, reps, rest, per-exercise rationale, sequencing roles, the session-level stimulus/target_adaptation/design_rationale, and the strength/conditioning/mobility stimulus distribution.

injury_state_used.notes: Feeling better today. Less stiffness. Still cautious on deep flexion.
